text.title: playwright-automation Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for playwright-automation as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Using CSS selectors from codegen output (.btn-primary, #form-submit) without converting to role locators.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Codegen generates CSS as a lowest-common-denominator fallback. These selectors die when styling changes, causing flaky tests that have nothing to do with functionality.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Ban it via CI lint rule. Adds 2 seconds per occurrence, scales with suite size, and masks actual timing problems. Use locator auto-wait or expect(locator).toBeVisible({timeout}).

failure-mode.description: Agents broadening selectors when getByRole() throws "strict mode violation: multiple elements".
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: The correct fix is narrowing with { name: 'specific label' }. Broadening picks an arbitrary element and silently tests the wrong thing.

failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: auth.json contains session tokens. Add to .gitignore and pull from secret store at test time. Trace artifacts may also contain PII; gate behind staging-only.

failure-mode.description: Codegen-generated CSS selectors used without converting to role/text locators.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Codegen generates CSS as a lowest-common-denominator fallback. Force agents to rewrite to role/text after recording.

failure-mode.description: WebKit headless on Linux has minor parity gaps with macOS Safari.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Run WebKit tests in CI on Linux but validate critical flows against real macOS Safari before release.

failure-mode.description: Trace viewer artifacts blow up CI: a 5-min run can produce 200MB.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Use trace: 'on-first-retry', not 'on'. This captures diagnostics only when tests fail.
